name: Chilies En Nogales (Stuffed Chilies with Waln
recipeIngredient: 1 large Green pepper or 2 small poblanos
2 medium Jalapeno peppers, washed and seeded
1 cup kidney beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the broiler.
2. Place the pepper on a foil-lined baking tray. Broil 2 inches from the heat for 5 minutes, turn and broil another 5 minutes.
3. Process the walnuts in a food processor until coarsely chopped.
4. In a small bowl, mix the sour cream and skim milk together until smooth. Add the walnuts, cinnamon, and sugar, and blend together. Set aside.
5. Chop the onion in the food processor. With the blades running, add the Jalapeno peppers and garlic. Stop the processor and add the beans and cumin. Process until coarsely chopped.
6. Heat the olive oil in a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the bean mixture and saute for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and raisins and saute for another 2 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
7. Remove the peppers from the broiler, cut them in half, and remove the seeds. Return the peppers to the tray. Fill each pepper with the bean mixture.
8. Place the peppers on individual plates, spooning sauce over the top, and serve with rice.
Nutritional information per serving: 364 calories; 16g protein, 47g carbohydrates, 15g fat (35%), 9.6g fiber, 22mg cholesterol, 740mg sodium
Exchanges: .1 milk, 2 vegetable, .5 fruit, 1.9 bread, .4 meat, 2.3 fat
